ddls:
CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "frequency_of_wonder" TEXT /* Frequency of feeling wonder about the universe (e.g., At least once a week) */,
  "once_a_week" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who feel wonder at least once a week */,
  "once_or_twice_a_month" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who feel wonder once or twice a month */,
  "several_times_a_year" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who feel wonder several times a year */,
  "seldom_never" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who feel wonder seldom or never */,
  "dont_know" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who do not know */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Total number of respondents for the given frequency */,
  "survey_version" TEXT /* Version of the survey */,
  "date_submitted" TEXT /* Date when the response was submitted */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to submit the response */,
  "survey_duration" INTEGER /* Duration in seconds taken to complete the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_frequency_of_wonder FOREIGN KEY ("frequency_of_wonder") REFERENCES survey_questions ("question_text")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the respondent (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the respondent (e.g., High School, Bachelor's, Master's) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the respondent (e.g., USA, UK, India) */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the survey response ID */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the respondent */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the respondent */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the respondent */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey question */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the survey question */,
  "question_type" TEXT /* Type of the survey question (e.g., Multiple Choice, Yes/No) */,
  "question_order" INTEGER /* Order in which the question is presented in the survey */,
  "question_category" TEXT /* Category of the question (e.g., Attitude, Demographic) */,
  "question_version" TEXT /* Version of the question */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the system */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last date the user logged in */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
